title: Business and Operations Consultants – Banking, Insurance, Life & Pensions
description:
Would you like to work for an independent consultancy alongside experienced colleagues, where a relaxed atmosphere is combined with commitment, professionalism, and a sustainable work–life balance?
 



  





 Midagon is a Nordic, independent management and IT consultancy specialising in leading complex transformation projects and programmes. We are now looking for senior Business and Operations Consultants to join our Banking & Insurance practice – supporting clients across Banking, Insurance, Life & Pensions.
 



  





 You will be based at one of our offices in Stockholm or Örebro, and we offer a hybrid working model. Naturally, some days will be spent on-site with clients.
 



  





**The Role** 




 As a Business and Operations Consultant, you will drive transformation and development for our clients – either as part of their team or in a leading role. You act as an independent advisor, helping clients translate strategy and target operating models into tangible change. Our assignments typically include:
 


* Business and operational analysis
* Target operating model development and customer journey design
* Process development and operational efficiency
* Digitalisation, automation and data‑driven development
* Project, programme and transformation management
* Roles such as Product Manager, Product Owner, Epic Owner, Project Manager or Transformation Lead



  





 You will join a senior team with deep expertise across Banking, Insurance, Life & Pensions, where collaboration, knowledge sharing and professionalism are central to how we work.
 



 We are looking for several profiles – which one are you?
 



  





**Consultant within Insurance, Life & Pensions** 




 You have many years of experience within the insurance sector and are used to working strategically with business and operational development. You may bring experience in:
 


* Business analysis, target operating models and process development
* Customer journey design and operational efficiency
* Agile development combined with project and change management
* Data‑driven development, automation and AI‑related initiatives
* Roles such as Business Analyst, Product Owner, Project Manager or Transformation Lead



  





**Consultant within Banking & Payments** 




 You have solid experience from the banking or payments sector and thrive in complex environments where you lead development and change. You may bring experience in:
 


* Business analysis and process development within payment solutions
* Target operating models and digitalisation
* Agile development combined with traditional project and change management
* Data‑driven development and an interest in AI and automation
* Roles such as Product Manager, Product Owner, Epic Owner, Project Manager or Transformation Lead



  





**We believe that you:** 



* Have several years of consulting experience within Banking, Insurance, Life & Pensions
* Are confident leading and driving work across different organisational levels
* Communicate clearly and enjoy working close to the client’s business
* Are fluent in both Swedish and English



  





 To thrive with us, you combine enthusiasm and pragmatism with a structured and methodical approach. Experience in leadership and change management is essential for success in the role.
 



  





**What We Offer** 




 You will join a growing practice and work on assignments that truly develop you – together with engaged and experienced colleagues who are passionate about creating value in complex transformation environments. You will be part of a senior consulting team where professionalism, collaboration and quality are a natural part of everyday life.
 



  





 We work with digital transformations within business development, project and programme management, and various specialist areas. Assignments are varied and challenging, and you will have significant scope to influence both delivery and ways of working. Our culture is characterised by low hierarchy, engagement, and a sustainable balance between work and private life. Here, you will have freedom, trust, and colleagues who are always willing to share their expertise.
 



  





**Curious to know more?** 




 Submit your application today – we review candidates on an ongoing basis. If you have any questions, you are welcome to contact Jonna Rickard Lindberg, People Manager, at jonna.rickardlindberg@midagon.com.
 



 We look forward to receiving your application.
 



  





**About Midagon** 




 Midagon is a Nordic, independent management and IT consultancy specialising in delivering challenging transformation projects and programmes. We are more than 230 employees across the Nordics, with nearly 100 in Sweden. We work with both private and public sector clients, from global organisations to national companies. Assignments vary in scope, but what they share is that we help our clients succeed with their complex transformations – something reflected in our long standing client relationships and several framework agreements.
 



  





 Our experienced consultants work with digital transformations within business development, project and programme management, and various specialist areas. With us, you will find some of the most experienced and highly regarded consultants in the industry. This is confirmed by awards such as Sweden’s most satisfied customers (Radar) and several consecutive years as a certified Great Place to Work.

title: Management Consultant
description:
Ready to launch your career and work hands-on with innovative digital solutions and future-proof business models?
 **The Customer Experience Transformation team** 
 at
 **frog** 
 is seeking
 
 driven graduates to join our community in Stockholm.
 



  





 At Capgemini Invent, we believe difference drives change. As inventive transformation consultants, we blend our strategic, creative and scientific capabilities, collaborating closely with clients to deliver cutting-edge solutions. Join us to drive transformation tailored to our client's challenges of today and tomorrow. Informed and validated by science and data. Superpowered by creativity and design. All underpinned by technology created with purpose.
 



  





  





**YOUR ROLE** 




 As a consultant at frog, a part of Capgemini Invent, you’ll work in multidisciplinary teams to solve real business challenges for leading organizations. You’ll be a part of a collaborative and vibrant community where design, data and business insight come together to reimagine customer experiences and drive lasting transformation.
 



  





*In this role you will play a key role in:* 



* Helping develop strategies, design new business models, and implement solutions that deliver measurable impact and long-term growth.
* Working at the intersection for creativity, strategy and technology, you’ll bring bold ideas to life – from concept to execution.



  





  





**YOUR PROFILE** 



* **Educational background:** 
 You hold a master’s degree with strong academic results.
* **Recent graduate:** 
 You are graduating in 2026 or have recently graduated, with 0-1 year of relevant work experience since completing your degree.
* **Skills and mindset:** 
 You’re curious, analytical, and thrive in fast-paced environments. You enjoy solving complex challenges and are eager to learn and grow - both independently and as part of a team.
* **Language skills:** 
 You have excellent communication skills in both Swedish and English.
* **Additional merits:** 
 International and professional experiences, as well as involvement in extracurricular activities, are considered valuable assets.



  





  





**WHAT YOU’LL LOVE ABOUT WORKING HERE?** 



* **Diverse environment:** 
 Collaborative with market-leading clients and experienced consultants within Sweden, Finland & globally.
* **Career development:** 
 Experience a well-defined career path with support to develop into a successful consultant, including training and mentoring programs.
* **Collaborative culture:** 
 Benefit from our supportive teams, knowledge sharing, and comprehensive end-to-end capabilities.
* **Engaging workplace:** 
 Embrace a culture where employees shape their own future and enjoy a welcoming, social workplace with many exciting events and activities.



  





  





**NEED TO KNOW** 





 Start date and location
 




 You’ll join our Stockholm office during the fall of 2026 with readiness to travel and work on projects at our client sites.
 



  






 Application process
 




 Applications are reviewed continuously; apply early to secure your spot. Please submit your CV in
 **English** 
 along with your official transcripts from high school and university. Since the system only allows one upload field for grades (
 
 under “Cover Letter”
 
 ), we kindly ask you to
 **combine all your transcripts-** 
 high school, bachelor’s, and master’s- into a single document before uploading. Incomplete applications will not be considered.
 



  






 Next steps
 




 Our selection process includes several steps to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation:
 



 - CV & grade screening
 



 - Phone interview
 



 - Video interview (including a case)
 



 - On-site group case
 



 - Final interview
 



 - Offer
 



  





**As part of our recruitment process, identity verification will be conducted through inspection of your ID card, and background checks may be carried out as required.** 




  





**ABOUT CAPGEMINI** 




 Capgemini is an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology, and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of over 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end-to-end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering and business operations. The Group reported 2025 global revenues of €22.5 billion. Make it real |
 
 www.capgemini.com

title: Kraftsystemingenjör inom data och modellering
description:
Ett komplext elsystem som utvecklas under snabb takt ställer höga krav på datakvalitet och robusta systemstöd. Som kraftsystemingenjör inom data och modellering på driftavdelningen arbetar du med datamodellering och strukturerad dataförvaltning för att säkerställa tillförlitlig och långsiktigt hållbara data. Rollen ger möjlighet att fördjupa din kunskap om det nordiska kraftsystemet och bidra till Svenska kraftnäts uppdrag i energiomställningen genom att möjliggöra datadrivna beslut och ökad digitalisering.
   

  

**Om tjänsten**
 I rollen som kraftsystemingenjör inom data och modellering bidrar du till att säkerställa att verksamhetskritiska data är korrekt, tillgänglig och långsiktigt förvaltningsbar i ett allt mer komplext elsystem. Du arbetar med modellering av kraftsystemmodeller, data management och systemstöd för driftplanering och uppföljning, samt är med och utvecklar teamets förmåga inom strukturerad datahantering. I din roll möjliggör du ökad digitalisering och automation, samt bidrar till Svenska kraftnäts uppdrag i energiomställningen och utvecklingen av det nordiska kraftsystemet genom att skapa förutsättningar till datadrivenhet.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter:
   

  

* Modellera och vidareutveckla kraftsystemmodeller för driftplanering.
* Förvalta och utveckla systemstöd för nätdrift, inklusive datamodellering, kravställning, test och viss konfiguration.
* Säkerställa hög datakvalitet, interoperabilitet och långsiktig dataförvaltning.
* Bidra till struktur, arkitektur och arbetssätt inom data management.
* Stödja digitalisering och automation av driftens processer.
* Fungera som en länk mellan elkraft, data och IT.
* Bidra till robusta lösningar för informationsutbyte, både internt och externt, inklusive att tillgodose ökade krav på samordning inom EU.


 Du blir en del av enheten Nätdriftutveckling på avdelning Drift. Enheten bildades 2025 och ansvarar för att driva utvecklingsarbete för den operativa nätdriften. Här arbetar elva medarbetare med olika roller och kompetenser, bland annat verksamhetsutvecklare, processledare, produktägare och kraftsystemspecialister. Arbetet präglas av nära samarbete, kunskapsutbyte och många kontaktytor mot driftverksamheten och IT-organisationen.
   

  

**Om dig**
**Skallkrav:**
 För att lyckas i rollen är du analytisk och strukturerad med förmåga att se helheter och omsätta komplex information till väl genomtänkta lösningar. Du är nyfiken och har ett intresse för att förstå och utveckla verksamheten över tid. Du trivs i samarbete med andra och bidrar aktivt i gemensamma arbetsprocesser och forum. Samtidigt är du initiativtagande och målmedveten, samt tar ansvar för att driva frågor framåt när du ser behov eller förbättringsmöjligheter.
   

  

 Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Du har en civilingenjörsexamen eller mastersexamen från universitet eller högskola inom elkraft, energisystem, data/informationsteknologi eller motsvarande utbildning eller arbetslivserfarenheter som Svenska kraftnät bedömer likvärdig.
   

  

 Du har även:
   

  

* Flerårig arbetslivserfarenhet inom ett för rollen relevant område.
* Erfarenhet av arbete inom data engineering och/eller data management, såsom utveckling och förvaltning av datainfrastruktur samt arbete med datakvalitet, struktur, metadata, masterdata eller informationsmodeller.
* Mycket god förmåga att kommunicera, både skriftligt och muntligt, på svenska och engelska.


 Det är
 **meriterande** 
 om du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av verksamhetsutveckling och/eller förändringsarbete.
* Erfarenhet av arbete inom elbranschen, exempelvis elnät, produktion, drift eller elmarknad.
* Kunskap om elsystemets struktur, dataflöden och tekniska beroenden.
* Erfarenhet av kraftsystemmodellering i exempelvis CIMdesk, PSS/E eller Power Factory.


**Bra att veta**
* Tjänsten är placerad i Stockholm/Sundbyberg
* Möjlighet till distansarbete upp till 50 %
* Resor kan förekomma någon gång per år
* Sista ansökningsdag 15 maj 2026, vi använder urvalsfrågor istället för personligt brev.
* Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning
* I denna rekryteringsprocess tillämpas tester på slutkandidater
* Förmåner | Svenska kraftnät


 Vid frågor om tjänsten kontakta rekryterande chef Jonas Bergman, tel. 010 475 86 86. Vid frågor om rekryteringsprocessen kontakta ansvarig rekryterare Linda Mattsson, tel. 010 350 91 47. Fackliga representanter är Annika Ingeborn, SACO, 010 475 87 72 och Erica Midfjäll ST, 010 475 87 31. Du når oss också via e-post: fornamn.efternamn@svk.se
   

  

 Jobba här – bli en viktig del av vår energiframtid | Svenska kraftnät
   

  

 Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare i samband med rekrytering, vi har upphandlade avtal som gäller för Svenska kraftnät som statlig myndighet.
   

  

 Övrig information
   

  

 Vi ställer höga krav på våra medarbetares säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetenhet. Innan ett beslut om anställning fattas kommer en säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) att genomföras i de fall befattningen innebär hantering av säkerhetsskyddsklassificerade uppgifter eller deltagande i säkerhetskänslig verksamhet. Om anställningen avser befattning i säkerhetsklass kommer säkerhetsprövningen även att innefatta en registerkontroll och, i vissa fall, en särskild personutredning. För anställning på Svenska kraftnät kan det vara ett krav på svenskt medborgarskap i vissa befattningar. Med anställningen följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras. För mer information om säkerhetsprövning Svenska kraftnät arbetar för att främja mångfald och motverka diskriminering och ser gärna sökanden med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter till våra rekryteringar. Lär känna Svenska kraftnät och hur det är att arbeta hos oss: Lär känna oss på Svenska kraftnät

title: Team Manager - Procurement & Software Asset Management to Sweco IT
description:
*At Sweco, we plan and design the sustainable communities and cities of the future. To*
*support our growing and complex landscape, Sweco IT is now looking for a Team*
*Manager to our Software Asset Management team to help us build transparency, drive*
*compliance and ensure that our software assets support both our business needs and*
*our sustainability ambitions.*
**The Role**
 Sweco is looking to grow our Software Asset Management team by hiring a team
   

  

 manager and expanding the team's scope. We want an enthusiastic leader who can
   

  

 develop the team, update governance policies and procedures, and deliver strong
   

  

 commercial results working with our software and hardware providers.
   

  

 You will lead a team of specialist and work closely with colleagues in Sweco IT, finance,
   

  

 legal, privacy, and business stakeholders to ensure openness, compliance, and value
   

  

 from supplier relationships and contracts.
   

  

**Your Main Responsibilities**
* Lead and develop the team, improving work methods and processes
* Coordinate with IT units and key stakeholders to define scope and


 priorities
   

  

* Manage contract lifecycles, renewals, compliance, and risk
* Enhance data quality, contract systems, and financial reporting
* Drive major negotiations and support strategic sourcing initiatives
* Support in Due Diligence and Integration projects in the Sweco M&A


 process
   

  

* Continue to develop and apply AI in procurement and contract


 management
   

  

* Deliver actionable reports and present decision memos to senior


 leadership
   

  

**Qualifications**
* Experienced in IT procurement, vendor and contract management
* Skilled in licensing, SaaS, cloud services, and software acquisition
* Strong negotiation, contract, and commercial expertise
* Proficient in financial analysis, TCO, forecasting, and reporting
* Process improvement and data quality enhancement experience
* Collaborative with legal, finance, security, architecture, and executive


 teams
   

  

* Proven leadership experience, preferably within IT or Asset Management
* Interested in responsible AI and automation within business processes


**About You**
 You are a confident and supportive leader, who enjoys developing people and building
   

  

 strong teams. You are a flexible, proactive, and results-driven professional who
   

  

 combines excellent negotiation and commercial awareness with strong analytical and
   

  

 problem-solving abilities. You take ownership, drive initiatives forward, and are
   

  

 motivated by improving ways of working. At the same time, you stay attentive to details
   

  

 and ensure compliance without losing sight of the bigger picture.
   

  

 Previous leadership experience is highly valued, and you thrive in an environment where
   

  

 you can influence, challenge and contribute to continuous improvement.
   

  

**Why join Sweco?**
* A pleasant and dynamic working atmosphere in a growing and leading company.
* A purpose-driven company where your work contributes to meaningful societal


 impact
   

  

* A culture that values inclusion, innovation, and continuous learning
* Flexibility, development opportunities, and a work culture built on trust and


 autonomy
   

  

**Can you see yourself as a part of Sweco?**
 Then don’t hesitate to send us your application and CV via the link below. We will review
   

  

 applications ongoing, so make sure you apply as soon as possible.
   

  

 We do not accept applications via email, however if you have any questions regarding
   

  

 the role or the recruitment process, don’t hesitate to contact us. We will get back to all
   

  

 applicants after the recruitment process has been completed.
   

  

 Do you want to be part of shaping the future? Then we look forward to receiving your
   

  

 application!
   

  

**Contact Us**
 If you want to know more about the position or Sweco as a workplace, please contact
   

  

 Baard Einar Moe, Head of Engineering IT Solution, at baardeinar.moe@sweco.no.
   

  

 Background checks will be carried out on the final candidate as part of this recruitment
   

  

 process.
   

  

*Sweco plans and designs the sustainable communities and cities of the future. Together*
*with our customers, our 23,000 architects, engineers and other experts develop*
*solutions to manage urbanization, take advantage of the opportunities of digitalization*
*and make the societies of the future more sustainable. Sweco is the leading consulting*
*company in technology and architecture in Europe with a turnover of approximately SEK*
*29 billion. The company is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. For further information,*
*http //www.sweco.se*

title: Microsoft 365 Specialist till Sweco IT
description:
*Sweco planerar och utformar framtidens hållbara samhällen och städer.* 




  





*Tillsammans med våra kunder utvecklar våra 23 000 arkitekter, ingenjörer och andra experter lösningar för att hantera urbanisering, ta tillvara på digitaliseringens möjligheter och göra framtidens samhällen mer hållbara.* 




  





*Sweco är Europas ledande konsultföretag inom teknik och arkitektur med en omsättning på cirka 29 miljarder kronor. Bolaget är noterat på Nasdaq Stockholm.* 




  





**Om jobbet** 




 Microsoft 365 Management-teamet på Sweco IT söker en engagerad Microsoft 365-specialist med ett starkt intresse för Identity and Access Management-lösningar (IAM). Den här rollen erbjuder en dynamisk arbetsmiljö där du får bidra till innovativa projekt, implementera nya lösningar och ge expertstöd i strategisk planering.
 



  





 Du kommer att tillhöra enheten Infrastructure & Cloud inom Sweco IT och rapportera till Team Manager för Microsoft 365 Management. Vårt team består av specialister inom identitet, samarbete och e-post/meddelandetjänster, fördelade över fem europeiska länder. I takt med att Microsoft 365 snabbt växer inom Sweco finns det stora möjligheter att driva förändring och ligga i framkant med ny teknik.
 



  





**Arbetsuppgifter** 




 I rollen som Microsoft 365-specialist kommer du att arbeta i vårt Microsoft 365 Management-team med placering på vårt kontor i Stockholm. I ditt dagliga arbete kommer du att undersöka och implementera nya lösningar, delta i pågående projekt, hantera förfrågningar om nya tjänster samt agera rådgivare i strategisk planering i nära samarbete med övriga verksamheten inom Sweco.
 



  





 För den här rollen är dina personliga egenskaper viktigast, men Sweco söker en person med en gedigen teknisk bakgrund och god kunskap inom flera Office 365-tjänster och teknologier, inklusive Entra ID.
 



  





 Du kommer att arbeta proaktivt med att utveckla och förbättra processer, lösa problem samt ta fram och vidareutveckla dokumentation. Du blir en “go-to”-person för teknisk rådgivning och information, både inom Sweco IT och i verksamheten.
 



  





**Erfarenheter och egenskaper** 




  





**Vi ser gärna att du har:** 



* Minst 3 års relevant erfarenhet och gedigen förståelse för tekniska koncept.
* Gedigen teknisk bakgrund med starka kunskaper om Office 365 tjänster och teknologier, inklusive Entra ID.
* Erfarenhet av O365-migreringsarbete i en stor organisation är fördelaktigt men inte nödvändigt.
* Proaktiv i att utveckla och förbättra processer, lösa problem och skapa dokumentation.
* Utmärkta kommunikationsfärdigheter på engelska, för att ge teknisk rådgivning och information inom kundens IT och den bredare verksamheten.



  





**Entra / Azure / O365:** 



* Användar- och grupphantering.
* Hantering av applikationer och behörigheter, rollbaserad åtkomstkontroll.
* SSO-konfigurationer, SAML och oAuth-autentisering.
* Konfiguration av villkorlig åtkomst och MFA.
* Arbetskunskaper om GraphAPI (med Entra och behörighetshantering).
* Kunskaper om Active Directory och Entra ID Connect.
* Erfarenhet av Powershell-skript och SQL (meriterande).
* Prenumerations- och resurshantering i Azure (meriterande).



  





**Microsoft 365:** 



* Förstå Microsoft 365-roadmap och proaktivt identifiera, undersöka och implementera fördelaktiga funktioner.
* Felsök, konfigurera och underhåll O365:s tjänster.
* Samarbeta med Enterprise Architects i stora organisationsprojekt.
* Ge råd om applikationer och tjänster inom Office 365, demonstrera och dokumentera möjligheter och begränsningar.
* Hantera incidenter och arbeta med externa leverantörer och intressenter.



  





 Som person är du driven, proaktiv, trivs med att arbeta i ett högt tempo och tycker att det är roligt att lösa problem. Du har förmågan att tänka både kortsiktigt och långsiktigt och brinner för att leverera goda resultat. Du är också självmotiverad, målinriktad och har förmåga att ta fram praktiska lösningar på problem.
 



  





**Varför jobba hos Sweco?** 



* Möjligheter till professionell utveckling och olika utbildningsinsatser.
* En trevlig och dynamisk arbetsmiljö i ett växande och ledande företag.
* En bransch som genomgår en stor förändring i arbetssätt.
* En kultur som värdesätter inkludering, innovation och kontinuerligt lärande.
* Flexibelt arbete med frihet och ansvar samt ett konkurrenskraftigt förmånspaket.



  





**Övrigt** 




 Plats: Stockholm (Hybrid)
 



  





 Start: Enl. överenskommelse
 



  





 Bakgrundskontroller kommer att genomföras på slutkandidaten som en del av denna rekryteringsprocess.
 



  





**Ansökan** 




 I den här rekryteringen samarbetar Sweco med D-Source. Vill du veta mer, kontakta ansvarig rekryteringskonsult robert.tse@d-source.se.
 



  





 Vi kan tyvärr inte ta emot ansökningar via mail då vi värnar om din personliga integritet.
 



  





 Vi arbetar med löpande urval av kandidater och kan komma att tillsätta tjänsten så fort vi hittat rätt person. Skicka därför in din ansökan så snart som möjligt!

title: Accountant
description:
**We are expanding our Finance team with an Accountant at our office in Sweden!** 




  





 As an Accountant at Fast Track, this role sits at the core of the finance function, responsible for ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and timeliness of the company’s financial operations and reporting. The position plays a critical part in maintaining robust accounting practices, supporting strategic decision-making through high-quality financial insights, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.
 



  





 Working closely with cross-functional teams and senior stakeholders, the role goes beyond transactional accounting to contribute to financial planning, performance analysis, and continuous improvement of processes. It also supports group-level reporting and consolidation activities, ensuring alignment across entities and enabling a clear, consolidated view of financial performance.
 



  





 This is a hands-on position requiring strong attention to detail, ownership of key accounting processes, and the ability to translate financial data into meaningful business insights.
 



  





  





**Your responsibilities as an Accountant at Fast Track would include:** 




  





**Financial Operations & Transaction Management** 



* Oversee and manage accounts payable, including issuing payments and maintaining accurate transaction records
* Handle day-to-day bookkeeping activities
* Perform regular bank reconciliations, ensuring accuracy and integrity of financial data



  





**General Ledger & Accounting Control** 



* Maintain, update, and review general ledger accounts for accuracy and completeness
* Prepare and post journal entries, including accruals
* Ensure proper account classification and reconciliation across all ledgers



  





**Financial Close & Reporting** 



* Support month-end, quarter-end, and year-end closing processes
* Ensure timely and accurate financial reporting
* Prepare financial statements, including income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow reports
* Provide insights and recommendations to support management decision-making



  





**Budgeting, Forecasting & Financial Analysis** 



* Assist in the preparation and monitoring of budgets and forecasts
* Produce variance analysis and provide commentary on financial performance
* Support revenue projections and cash flow planning
* Deliver ad-hoc financial analysis to support business decisions



  





**Compliance, Tax & Audit** 



* Ensure compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements
* Prepare and submit tax filings (e.g. VAT)
* Support audit preparation and liaise with auditors
* Stay up to date with changes in accounting regulations and best practices



  





**Group Accounting & Consolidation** 



* Support group accounting processes, including financial consolidation
* Manage intercompany transactions and recharging processes



  





**Process Improvement & Controls** 



* Identify and implement improvements to accounting processes and internal controls
* Enhance efficiency, accuracy, and scalability of financial operations



  





**Stakeholder Collaboration & Business Support** 



* Collaborate with internal teams (finance, payroll, HR, marketing) to ensure alignment
* Partner with the Head of Finance on financial planning and strategic analysis



  





  





**Job Requirements** 




  




* Minimum of 4 years of experience in accounting in Sweden, with a demonstrated track record in a similar role.
* Strong knowledge of accounting principles (IFRS) and Swedish tax laws.
* Proven ability to work independently and meet deadlines.
* Excellent attention to detail with a high degree of accuracy in work.
* Strong communication skills in Swedish and English, both written and verbal.
* Advanced proficiency in accounting software (preferably QuickBooks and Visma) and use of spreadsheets.
* Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills.
* Ability to work effectively as part of a team while also being capable of working independently.
* Tech-Driven: You are curious about how emerging tools can improve Finance operations. You enjoy experimenting with new technology, quickly picking up systems and workflows, and finding smarter, more efficient ways to get work done. You stay current with digital trends and are energised by continuous change



  





  





**Working at Fast Track** 




 Fast Track is a disruptive technology company, recognised as the iGaming industry CRM leader. Fast Track provides a new way of working, enabling teams to focus on innovation and growth. We are a tight unit with a strong culture, and our leadership in tech and the product has attracted high-performance individuals. Fast Track works with hundreds of companies worldwide, with offices in Malta, Sweden, Spain, and the United States, and has been certified as a Great Place To Work®️
 



  





  





**Perks and Benefits of Working at Fast Track in Stockholm, Sweden** 




  





**Great Place to Work® certified** 
 -Officially recognised for our commitment to building an engaging, high-trust culture.
 



**Centrally located office at Vasagatan** 
 - Work in the conveniently located Vasagatan, central Stockholm
 



**Pension contribution** 
 - We contribute to your government Pension Scheme
 



**Mobile coverage** 
 - Receive an iPhone on a paid mobile plan
 



**Be part of our yearly Growth Summit** 
 - Join our global team for an inspiring event to connect, collaborate, and celebrate together
 



**Wellbeing benefit** 
 - We sponsor your well-being activities, such as gym memberships or fitness classes, to support your wellbeing up to 5000 SEK
 



**Private health insurance** 
 - Comprehensive coverage for you through Länsförsäkringar.
 



**Mental wellbeing support** 
 - 24/7 access to mental health support
 



**Apple equipment and Google Workspace** 
 - Best-in-class MacBooks and all the tools you need to do your best work
 



**Daily breakfast** 
 - Start your morning with a complimentary, healthy breakfast at the office.

title: Project Controller
description:
**About the Position** 




  





 As Project Controller at Sting, you are an important part of the financial backbone that keeps our projects on track and our reporting sharp. You will work closely with - and report directly to - our CFO, supporting the financial and operational health of one of Europe's leading startup accelerators.
 



 This is a role for someone who loves structure, but thrives in an environment that doesn't always follow a straight line. At Sting, we're a small, fast-moving team - which means some days are calm and analytical, others are dynamic and demanding. You adapt naturally to both.
 



 You're not just a numbers person. You're someone who understands that great financial control is a service to the organisation - and you bring both the rigour and the collaborative spirit to make that real.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




  





**Project Controlling & Stakeholder Support -** 
 You track project timelines and financial progress, and ensure that key stakeholders always have the data they need to make informed decisions. You are proactive in flagging risks before they become problems, and you keep projects on schedule and within budget.
 



**Budgeting & Forecasting -** 
 You develop and maintain project budgets, establish financial baselines, and model future costs. Your forecasts are reliable, well-documented, and communicated with clarity.
 



**Cost Control & Analysis -** 
 You monitor project spend, analyse revenue streams, and manage financial risk to protect profitability. Your analytical mindset means you don't just report what happened - you help the organisation understand why, and what to do next.
 



**External Reporting -** 
 You are an important part of the team that produces accurate and timely project reports to external parties such as Tillväxtverket, Vinnova and EU. You have a solid understanding of EU state aid regulations (statsstödsregelverket) and ensure compliance in all relevant reporting.
 



**Process Improvement -** 
 You don't settle for "the way it's always been done." You actively look for opportunities to improve internal processes - whether through reporting automation, better data flows, or smarter operational insights. You aim to leave things better than you found them.
 



  





**Qualifications & Experience** 




  




* Proven experience in controlling, auditing, or quality control — you know how to maintain high standards in financial processes and project management
* Solid understanding of EU state aid regulations (statsstödsregelverket)
* Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and the ability to draw meaningful insights from complex data
* Excellent communication skills in Swedish and English — both written and spoken — with a professional and clear style
* A collaborative team player who also works effectively independently
* You are comfortable working in a digital environment, have hands-on experience with AI automation tools, and can quickly adapt to new IT systems



  





**Ways of Working** 




  




* **Solution-oriented** 
 : You meet challenges with a problem-solving mindset, not a problem-listing one
* **Self-driven** 
 : You take ownership of your work and deliver without needing to be micromanaged
* **Flexible & agile** 
 : Priorities shift, tasks evolve, and no two weeks look the same — you're comfortable with that
* **Improvement-minded** 
 : You're always looking for ways to make processes smarter, faster, and more effective
* **Collaborative** 
 : You build strong working relationships and understand that great work is rarely done alone



  





**What Sting Offers** 




  




* A meaningful role at the heart of one of the world's leading startup accelerators
* A small, skilled, and dedicated team where your work genuinely matters
* A fast-paced and entrepreneurial environment where you can grow and shape your role over time
* The opportunity to work alongside exceptional founders who are solving the world's biggest challenges
* A culture built on trust, curiosity, and the drive to keep improving

title: Vikarierande Webbkommunikatör till Swedavia
description:
Vill du vara med och påverka Swedavias digitala kommunikation? Nu söker vi en självgående och datadriven webbkommunikatör till ett vikariat på marknadsavdelningen – en central roll där du får utveckla innehållet i våra externa kanaler.
   

  

 Tjänsten är ett föräldravikariat med start så snart som möjligt och som sträcker sig till sista januari 2027.
   

  

**Vad innebär rollen?**
 Arbetet syftar till att ansvara för och bidra till att Swedavias kommersiella digitala kanaler har relevant, kvalitetssäkrat och målgruppsanpassat innehåll som stödjer verksamhetens mål. Detta görs genom att planera, producera, publicera och utveckla digitalt innehåll i nära samarbete med interna och externa aktörer.
   

  

 Du kommer bland annat att:
   

  

* Tillsammans med chef för kommersiella digitala kanaler och kollegor ansvara för inriktning, samordning och kvalitetssäkring av innehåll i Swedavias externa digitala kanaler, inklusive webb, app och chatbotar
* Planera, skapa, publicera och vidareutveckla innehåll i våra digitala kanaler
* Arbeta kontinuerligt med UX-förbättringar och konverteringsoptimering (CRO) baserat på insikter från webbanalys
* Arbeta nära informationsägare och andra interna aktörer för att säkerställa enhetliga budskap och korrekt innehåll enligt gällande lagkrav
* Samverka med IT, leverantörer och utvecklingsteam för att säkerställa fungerande publiceringsflöden och kvalitetssäkrade leveranser


 Bidra till och samverka kring digital kommunikation kopplad till CRM, Marketing Automation och Affiliate Marketing
   

  

**Vem är du?**
 Du är strukturerad och självgående och trivs i en roll där du kombinerar operativt arbete med ett strategiskt perspektiv. Du planerar, prioriterar och driver ditt arbete framåt med stor ansvarskänsla, samtidigt som du är lyhörd och prestigelös i samarbetet med andra.
   

  

 Du har en stark kommunikativ förmåga och kan omsätta komplex information till tydligt, målgruppsanpassat och användarfokuserat innehåll. Med stöd av data arbetar du kontinuerligt för att förbättra innehåll, flöden och användarupplevelse i digitala kanaler.
   

  

 Du är nyfiken, flexibel och trivs i en föränderlig miljö där teknik och arbetssätt utvecklas, och samarbetar obehindrat med verksamhet, IT och externa leverantörer.
   

  

 Vi ser att du har:
   

  

* Eftergymnasial utbildning inom kommunikation, digitala medier eller motsvarande erfarenhet
* Minst 3–5 års erfarenhet av arbete med digital kommunikation/webb
* Mycket god erfarenhet av Content Management och arbete i CMS (gärna Optimizely/Episerver)
* Erfarenhet av att skriva både informativa och konverterande innehåll för digitala kanaler
* God förståelse för UX, användarflöden, konverteringsoptimering (CRO) och SEO
* Erfarenhet av webbanalys, gärna Google Analytics 4, och att arbeta datadrivet
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift


 Det är meriterande om du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av CRM och Marketing Automation
* Kunskap eller praktisk erfarenhet av Affiliate Marketing
* Kunskaper inom grafisk formgivning och arbete i Adobe Creative Cloud
* Erfarenhet av webbutvecklingsprojekt och kravställning


**Vad erbjuder vi dig?**
 Vi erbjuder ett omväxlande arbete i en samhällsviktig verksamhet där du får möjlighet att påverka och utveckla Swedavias digitala kanaler. Du blir en del av en inkluderande arbetsgemenskap som uppmuntrar nytänkande och samarbete.
   

  

 Vid frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Julia Schuchardt, julia.schuchardt@swedavia.se
   

  

**Sista ansökningsdag är söndag 17 maj, urval sker löpande och annonsen kan stängas tidigare så tveka inte att söka redan idag!**
**Hur är det att arbeta på Swedavia?**
 Vårt fokus är att möjliggöra hållbart resande och uppmuntra nytänkande för att skapa en välkomnande, trygg och inkluderande arbetsgemenskap där du kan växa och göra verklig skillnad – läs mer här! Jobba hos oss | Om Swedavia
   

  

 För en objektiv och rättvis rekrytering använder vi arbetspsykologiska tester som en del av urvalsprocessen.
   

  

 Då flygplatser är en säkerhetsklassad verksamhet genomgår samtliga kandidater som går vidare i rekryteringsprocessen en bakgrundskontroll. För vissa tjänster även en säkerhetsprövning. Swedavia är även en drogfri arbetsplats med nolltolerans mot droger. Drogtester genomförs före anställning och sker slumpmässigt under anställningen.

title: Programadministratör
description:
**Techster Solutions** 
 befinner i dagsläget sig i en spännande tillväxtfas och söker nu fler kollegor, inledningsvis dig med minst
 **3+** 
 års erfarenhet och som kan stärka teamet.
   

  

 Du kan förvänta dig en omväxlande miljö, dagarna kan se väldigt olika ut - men en sak är säkert, här får du använda dina kompetenser och sätter själv gränsen för hur mycket du vill utvecklas. Som ett kunskapsbolag är det viktigt för oss att våra specialister delar med sig av sin kompetens och får kollegorna att växa.
   

  

**Rollbeskrivning** 
 :
   

  

 I rollen som programadministratör både för ledningsprojekt som stationsprojekt har man ett viktigt ansvar i att stödja och avlasta programledaren med framför allt administrativa sysslor och bidrar därmed till resurseffektivt genomförande av projekten. Rollen innebär stort eget ansvar att självständigt planera och utföra sitt arbete inom de arbetsområden som delegerats av programledaren. Rollen kräver en förmåga att kunna strukturera upp arbetet för att hålla ordning och reda på programmets dokumentation genom hela livscykeln. Arbetsuppgifterna kan variera beroende av programmet karaktär och behov.
   

  

**Roll och ansvar:**
 Rollen som programadministratör kräver att man är van vid att arbeta enligt tydliga processer. Man ska kunna planera sitt arbete väl och ha lätt för att hålla tidplaner. Att ha ett bra samarbete med såväl interna som externa kollegor med ett prestigelöst fokus. Förmågan att ta initiativ och ha lätt för att skapa nya relationer värderas högt i verksamheten.
   

  

**I uppdraget ingår följande arbetsuppgifter:**
* Skapa ordning och reda genom strukturerad och noggrann administration med hög servicegrad
* Administrera interna och externa möten såsom programlednings- och styrgruppsmöten samt skriva protokoll från dessa
* Skapa och upprätthålla effektiva rutiner avseende rapportering och uppföljning samt bistå och genomföra rapportering för programmet
* Diarieföra programmets dokumentation och beslutsdokument från projekten
* Administration i stödsystem för dokumentation, ekonomi och projektrapportering
* Arbete med beslut, ärende - och dokumentationshantering
* Ansvara för helheten vad gäller programmets administration och interna kommunikationsflöden
* Stötta programledaren i att utveckla/effektivisera programmets administration och interna styrning.


**KRAV** 
 :
   

  

* Avslutad gymnasieutbildning
* Minst 3-års arbetslivserfarenhet av att ha självständigt arbetat som program/projektadministratör i en stödjande roll till projektledare eller programledare med minst 10 parallella projekt
* Erfarenhet av att ha arbetat i stora anläggnings- och infrastrukturprojekt med flera interna och externa intressenter, med höga krav på tidplaner och säkerhet.
* Erfarenhet av att ha arbetat i ärende- och dokumentationshanteringssystem


**MERIT**
* Eftergymnasial utbildning om minst tre år
* Minst 3-års arbetslivserfarenhet av offentlig verksamhet
* Erfarenhet av att ha arbetat i projektverktyget Antura eller liknande projektverktyg


**Ansökan**
 Tror du att du är
 **Techster Solutions** 
 nästa stjärna? Tveka då inte att skicka in din ansökan redan nu. Urval sker löpande och tillträde sker enligt överenskommelse.
   

  

 Arbetsplats och kultur
   

  

 Vi är ett entreprenördrivet IT företag, med både produktkunskap och försäljning (Techster AB) och konsultativa tjänster (Techster Solutions AB). Det innebär att vi är mycket öppna för nya tekniska lösningar, kunskapsutbyte och ständigt lärande. Att ha roligt och samtidigt behålla den familjära andan utan hierarkier är lika viktigt.
   

  

 Vi vill skapa det konsultbolag som du alltid velat jobba för, men kanske inte hittat ännu. Redan idag har vi en blandning av seniora och yngre medarbetare, såväl som män och kvinnor. Det vill vi fortsätta att bygga på. Så välkommen med din ansökan!

title: Vendor Manager
description:
Shape the circular future as our next Vendor Manager!
 



  





**Are you an accomplished professional in product/vendor management? We invite you to leverage your expertise as our new Vendor Manager in Stockholm and make a tangible impact!** 




  





 Our new
 **Vendor Manager** 
 is a proactive individual with a strong business acumen. As a key player on our team, your role is to develop the partnerships with our main suppliers in Sweden (Dell, HP, and Lenovo), and some other selected key suppliers.
 



  





 Foxway offers a stimulating and inclusive work environment where your role as a manager is crucial for the team's success and individual growth. We aim to create a workplace where all employees are encouraged to develop and reach their full potential.
 



  





**What you will do as a Vendor Manager:** 




 As Vendor Manager, you will be responsible for vendor management activities in Sweden, with a strong focus on commercial performance and operational alignment. Your responsibilities include:
 


* Managing and developing relationships with OEMs and key suppliers.
* Executing vendor and OEM strategies aligned with Foxway’s overall objectives.
* Driving product transitions and lifecycle changes in close collaboration with internal stakeholders.
* Optimizing product portfolios and commercial terms.
* Ensuring compliance with supplier requirements and contractual obligations.
* Working closely with Sales to support a competitive and consistent customer offering.



  





**Who we are looking for someone who** 
 :
 



 Has a commercially minded professional who demonstrates analytical capabilities and sound business judgement. The ideal candidate brings:
 


* A minimum of 5 years’ experience in vendor management, product management, or a similar commercial role, preferably within the OEM or IT ecosystem.
* Master’s degree in engineering, Finance, Business or Supply Chain Management, or other Education relevant to excel at Foxway.
* Proven ability to manage complex supplier relationships and negotiations.
* Strong decision‑making and problem‑solving skills.
* Fluency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken.



  





 This role requires on-site presence at least four (4) days per week in Stockholm (Solna) office, with flexibility to travel to enable close collaboration with stakeholders both internally and externally.
 



  





**To thrive and succeed with us…** 




 You should have the ability to analyze and make decisions. You should be passionate about leadership and development and have a drive for continuous improvement. It is an advantage if you thrive in a fast-paced environment, take on challenges readily, and enjoy learning new things. We also hope that you enjoy working in a dynamic environment where you show great commitment and initiative!
 



  





**Why join Foxway?** 



* You have the opportunity to contribute to a more circular and sustainable future.
* A workplace where we have fun, challenge each other, and bring out the best in one another.
* Opportunities for both personal growth and career opportunities within the company.
* We also ensure competitive conditions and good pension and insurance arrangements for our people - and employee discount in our own online store!



  





 We are reviewing applications on an ongoing basis so if this sounds like an opportunity you want to pursue, apply quickly!

title: Service Technican
description:
Your Job Tasks
   

  

 We offer a stimulating work environment where you will have the opportunity to handle a variety of tasks, including servicing, troubleshooting, and repairing cranes and hooklifts. Perform preventive maintenance and communicate mechanical issuies with customers.
   

  

 As a service technician, you will work both in teams and independently on tasks related to service, repairs, and maintenance. It is essential that you are responsible, solution-oriented, and flexible
   

  

**Your Qualifications**
 We look forward to working with someone who has prior experience in workshop and service operations and possesses solid knowledge of electronics, mechanics, hydraulics, troubleshooting diagnostics
   

  

 At Palfinger, you will work in a dynamic environment where customer interaction is a key part of your responsibilities. Therefore, it is important that you are outgoing and enjoy providing excellent service.
   

  

 What we offer
   

  

 We provide exciting and challenging workdays with excellent opportunities for personal development. Additionally, we offer competitive salaries and health insurance. Our company places significant emphasis on workplace safety, employee satisfaction, and overall well-being.
   

  

 During your employment, we offer continuous training to help you grow in your career field, along with job security and excellent benefits.
   

  

 This is a full-time position.
   

  

 Welcome to Palfinger—we look forward to having you on our team!
   

  

 Are you interested in the position and still have questions? Please do not hesitate to contact us.

title: Engagement Partner – Data, Analytics & AI
description:
LTM — a Larsen & Toubro company — is an AI-centric global technology services company and the Business Creativity partner to the world’s largest and most disruptive enterprises. We bring human insights and intelligent systems together to help clients create greater value at the intersection of technology and domain expertise. Our capabilities span integrated operations, transformation, and business AI — enabling new ways of working, new productivity paradigms, and new roads to value.
 



  





 Together with over 87,000 employees across 40 countries and our global network of partners, LTM owns business outcomes for our clients, helping them not just outperform the market, but to Outcreate it.
 



  





**Job Summary** 




  





 We are looking for an experienced
 **Engagement Partner** 
 in
 **Data, Analytics & AI** 
 – working with a strong BFSI domain understanding
 



  





**Client Relationship & Revenue Protection** 




  




* Establish and maintain strong relationships with client stakeholders across CDO, LOB Data Leaders and data teams
* Act as a trusted onsite advisor – anticipate risks to existing programs, manage escalations early and protect renewals/expansions



  





**Opportunity Sensing & Pipeline Creation** 




  




* Proactively identify opportunities by onsite presence: attending relevant forums, "walking the floors", listening for pain points and translating them into structured opportunities
* Identify opportunities proactively
* Work with Sales leader on RFP’s and client requests
* Coordinate internal teams (delivery, architecture, partner alliances, CoE) to respond rapidly to client needs



  





**Consultative Solutioning (Data + AI + Experience)** 




  




* Ability to produce point-of-view (PoV), solution outlines, and “option A/B/C” approaches for client challenges
* Experience in setting up data ecosystems for AI, Data Modernization, Data Governance & Management & BI Consolidation solutions
* Partner with Hyperscalers on solutioning
* Prior involvement in data product operating models, domain data marts, or analytics modernization programs



  





**Must have skills** 




  




* Data, Analytics, AI, BFSI Domain, Data Architecture, Data Modeling, Data Tech Solutioning, Post Go Live Solutioning, Data Modernization, Data Migration, Data Governance & Management, FinOps Principles, Snowflake, Fabric, AWS.



  





**Others** 




  




* Willingness to travel in the Nordics



  





**What You Can Expect** 




  




* Become a part of one of the fastest growing IT-services companies in the world
* Organization driven by team spirit and technology
* Entrepreneurial culture where you can influence and grow
* Be in charge of your own career
* Work life balance
* Market benefits

title: Senior Web Developer - Growth
description:
**Why This Role Matters**
 Funnel’s website is our primary growth channel. It’s where potential customers first understand our value, build trust, and decide whether to take the next step. In this role, you’ll own and evolve that experience- building high-quality web interactions that directly impact conversion, lead quality, and revenue.
   

  

 You’ll work on a modern, AI-powered product used by marketers and brands worldwide, and your work will shape how it’s discovered, understood, and adopted. You’ll also work with colleagues who actively leverage AI tooling to ideate, design, code, and ship faster.
   

  

**About The Role**
 This is a hands-on role for a frontend-focused engineer who wants to work close to the business impact of what they build. You’ll take ownership of Funnel’s marketing site as a product building, iterating, and continuously improving it through experimentation.
   

  

 You’ll work closely with marketing, product, and revenue teams to ship quickly, test ideas, and turn traffic into meaningful customer conversations.
   

  

 You will work closely to these wonderfully smart and kind people: Marketa, Thomas, and Elias.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Build and maintain Funnel’s marketing website using modern frontend technologies
* Develop high-quality, performant, and accessible web experiences that reflect our product and brand
* Run and ship experiments on a continuous basis to improve conversion rates and user engagement
* Collaborate with marketing and product teams to launch new pages, campaigns, and features within days, not weeks
* Set up and work with tracking (e.g. events, funnels) to measure performance and evaluate experiment outcomes
* Iterate on landing pages, signup flows, and interactive elements based on data and user insights
* Use modern AI tools to accelerate how you design, build, and iterate on web experiences
* Explore and implement AI-powered features or experiences (e.g. interactive demos, personalization, or content generation) where it creates real user value
* Contribute to improving how we work with experimentation, tooling, and web performance


**Skills & Experience We’re Looking For**
* At least 5 years of experience working in a web or frontend development role/s
* Strong frontend engineering skills with experience in React, TypeScript, HTML, and CSS
* Experience building and maintaining modern, production-grade web applications
* Hands-on experience working with experimentation, A/B testing, or data-driven improvements in a web or product environment
* Comfortable using data (e.g. event tracking, funnels, analytics tools) to guide decisions and evaluate impact
* Experience working with APIs, integrations, and backend services (e.g. Node.js, cloud environments like AWS)
* Experience and excitement leveraging AI tools in day-to-day development workflows (e.g. code generation, debugging, prototyping), and curious about how AI can enhance user experiences
* Ability to turn ideas into shipped features quickly, with a focus on quality, performance, and iteration


**Nice to Haves**
* Experience working in growth or marketing-focused teams
* Familiarity with analytics and experimentation tools
* Knowledge of SEO and web performance optimization
* Experience from fast-growing B2B SaaS companies or agencies


**Our Ways of Working**
 We organize our work around domains and workstreams instead of traditional teams. Domains define clear areas of ownership, while workstreams form around shared goals and outcomes. This way of working helps us reduce dependencies, collaborate more naturally, and move faster with greater autonomy. We focus on delivering meaningful impact, learning through iteration, and continuously improving together. Read more about how we work →
   

  

 Good to know
   

  

* This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden
* We are an office-first company and work from the office at least 4 days per week. With flexibility of course!
* At this time, we can only consider applicants who:
+ Hold Swedish citizenship, or
+ Hold EU/EEA citizenship and are currently residing in Stockholm, or
+ Hold a valid Swedish work permit

* We do not offer relocation support for this role


 The Hiring Process
   

  

* Screening call – you get to know us, we get to know you! We're interested in hearing what you have been working on, and how your experience aligns with what we are looking for. We will share more context about the role, team and company and welcome any of your questions.
* Technical interviews – onsite deep dive into your technical experience, hands on coding and problem-solving approach
* Offer!


**About Funnel**
 Funnel is the leading Marketing Intelligence platform that empowers marketers to automatically integrate, attribute, measure, and report on data from over 600 marketing platforms.
   

  

 Trusted by more than 3,000 customers worldwide—including globally recognized brands such as Adidas, The Home Depot, Uber, Gong, Skechers, and Sony, as well as leading agencies like Havas, Power Digital, and Publicis—Funnel generated over $57M in ARR in 2024.
   

  

 Since its founding in 2014, Funnel has grown into a global team of over 300 employees, with offices in Stockholm, Boston, Hamburg, and Sydney. Funnel continues to feature in the FT 1000 list of Europe’s fastest-growing companies.
   

  

 Funnel is the leading Marketing Intelligence platform that empowers marketers to automatically integrate, attribute, measure, and report on data from over 500 marketing platforms.
   

  

 Trusted by almost 3,000 businesses worldwide, including globally recognized brands such as Adidas and Sony, Funnel generated over $70M in ARR in 2025 and continues to feature in the FT 1000 list of Europe’s fastest-growing companies.
   

  

 Since its founding in 2014, Funnel has grown into a global team of over 300 employees, with offices in Stockholm, Boston, Hamburg, and Sydney.

title: Buyer
description:
* Application Deadline: May 14, 2026


**Job Description**
 At Lyko, purchasing isn't a support function, it's how we grow. With over 87,000 products and more than 1,000 brand partners, our assortment is our biggest competitive advantage. Without the right suppliers, the right deals and the right people managing those relationships, we simply can't reach our goals as a company. That's not an exaggeration, it's just how we're built. We're in a phase where the decisions we make in purchasing today will shape what Lyko looks like tomorrow. Sounds like something that would tickle your fancy? Keep reading!
   

  

**Curious to learn more about Lyko?**
 We're on a mission to take over the world, at least the world of beauty 😉. We currently offer the widest selection of haircare and beauty products on the market and we're the leading beauty e-commerce company in the Nordics. With over 30 stores across Sweden, Norway and Finland, state-of-the-art production facilities, well-equipped warehouses, a robust 3PL solution, a growing B2B sales division and 16+ of our own brands. Maybe you've seen our new fabulous stores just opened in Kalmar and Charlottenberg. Make sure to check us out further on our career pages or maybe head out and check out our assortment!
   

  

**What You'll Do 💄**
 We're not looking for someone to just manage suppliers. We're looking for a sharp, curious and commercially driven partner who sees the bigger picture, dares to challenge the ordinary and wants to shape the future of beauty retail with us. As our Buyer, you'll own the full supplier relationship, from day-to-day collaboration to long-term strategic growth, working across all our markets and taking charge of some of our top-performing global brands. You'll act as the key link between our commercial teams and suppliers, making sure every campaign, contract and collaboration delivers value for all parties.
   

  

**In Other Words, You Will**
* Manage and develop relationships with key suppliers and brands
* Lead campaign activations and joint marketing initiatives
* Own contract negotiations and annual agreements — and push hard when it matters
* Follow up on KPIs and sales performance, and take action when needed
* Drive category growth through creativity, analysis and strong commercial insight
* Work closely with the cross-functional team to ensure assortment is up to date and stock align with campaign plans and category priorities


**Who You Are ✨**
 We're not just looking for a Buyer. We're looking for someone who understands that great supplier relationships don't just happen, they're negotiated, built and developed with real commercial intent. Someone who can mix creativity with commercial instinct, manage complex negotiations like a pro and bring fresh energy to one of Lyko's most dynamic teams.
   

  

 We would say that you're a natural relationship builder with sharp commercial instincts and a proven track record in negotiations. Someone who can switch between data and dialogue, just as comfortable crunching numbers in Excel as leading a tough supplier conversation with confidence and precision.
   

  

**You Probably Recognise Yourself Here**
* Experience in a buying or category role - the industry matters less than the skills you bring. What counts is that you're comfortable handling sometimes tough conversations and know how to win the supplier over with smart and innovative ideas that work for the both of you.
* You know how to build relationships and handle supplier conversations with confidence, even when they’re tough
* Structured, detail-oriented and reliable - people know that when you own something, it gets done
* Strong negotiation skills and a track record of driving supplier growth
* Skilled in Excel and comfortable working with data tools such as BI or similar
* Degree in business, purchasing or a related field
* Naturally proactive, structured and able to handle multiple priorities, we're a company in constant change, so a high degree of self-management and initiative will take you far in this role
* Fluent in English and Swedish


**About The Role And The Team 🤝**
 As a Buyer at Lyko, you will be a key part of our Purchasing team and report directly to the Global Category Manager. This is a permanent position with a 6 month probation period based in Stockholm, starting as soon as possible or at a mutually agreed date.
   

  

 We're actively investing in how we work in purchasing, and we're looking for someone who wants to grow with us, not just fill a seat. If you're at a point in your career where you know this world and want to bring that expertise to a progressive, fast-moving company with real ownership and a team that takes purchasing seriously as a business driver, then this is the right move for you!
   

  

**Apply now — no CV or cover letter needed.**
 Hey! Don’t hesitate to apply even though you don’t tick all the boxes on our (wish) list. We believe that who you are and what skills you have is more important than the number of years of experience ⭐
   

  

 If this sounds exciting and you're eager to apply - go ahead and do it ASAP! It’s quick and easy. No CV or cover letter needed, instead, we ask you to answer a few questions that are valuable for us in our selection process. Please apply as soon as possible, but no later than May 14th, 2026. ✨ If you have any questions about the role, reach out to Rebecka Appelblad, Talent Acquisition Partner at

title: Business Analyst| Group Credit Management
description:
**Job ID: 1772**
 Would you like to be part of a Nordic team, developing the credit processes for our customers? We are now looking for a Business Analyst to our Default Management Team.
   

  

 We are offering you an exciting opportunity to develop key capabilities at the heart of Nordea. Your work will require you to understand the connection between regulatory requirements, business processes and data to pro-actively drive initiatives and solve complex problems connected to Nordea’s credit risk process.
   

  

**About Our Team**
 Welcome to the Default Management team. We are driving the development for high risk credit processes (such as default management and early warnings). Our aim is to build sustainable processes and supporting tools that cater for automation and bring business value to our consumers. You will work closely with team members and relevant stakeholders to drive the implementation of new solutions and processes for business, regulatory and data requirements.
   

  

 Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.
   

  

**Main responsibilities in this role:**
* Building expertise of Nordea’s credit risk agenda, especially within the high risk area
* Be part of an agile structure, with focus on defining, prioritizing and driving analysis and change initiatives from initiation to successful delivery, in close collaboration with development teams, relevant stakeholders and management
* Ensuring our solutions meet business and regulatory requirements and maximize business value
* Identifying and actively proposing improvements to streamline the end to end credit processes and enable automation in the area


 You’ll join a diverse, multi-faceted and passionate team.
   

  

**Who You Are**
**Your background and skills include:**
* At least 3-5 years of experience driving change (from gathering business / regulatory requirements to implementation) in an agile environment
* You are strong in documenting and conceptualising requirements in complex environments and comfortable performing data analysis
* Furthermore, you ideally have experience working with and understanding credit risk or similar related regulations
* Have a professional level of English, both written and spoken
* Have excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, building strong relationships with stakeholders across the bank


**It would be ideal if you also:**
* Can effectively deliver and prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment
* Are self-going with a natural “can do” attitude, show ability to learn quickly and have a solution-oriented mindset supported by structured, analytical and creative thinking


**What We Offer**
**People** 
 come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.
   

  

**A culture** 
 that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.
   

  

**Hybrid working model** 
 – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.
   

  

**Diversity and inclusion** 
 are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.
   

  

 If this sounds like you, get in touch!
   

  

**Next steps**
 Submit your application no later than 17/05/2026. Please note that we may start interviews already during the application period. For more information, you’re welcome to contact Anuya Phadke, anuya.phadke@nordea.com.
   

  

**We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.**
**We build relationships.** 
 We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.
   

  

**We learn and develop.** 
 We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.
   

  

**We lead change.** 
 We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.
   

  

**We are Nordea.** 
 We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.
   

  

 For union information, please contact finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACONordea@nordea.com.

title: Performance Marketing Specialist
description:
**Lenus is looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist for our Stockholm office!** 




  





 Join the health revolution!
 



  





 At Lenus we are fundamentally energized by our shared vision: Making the world a healthier and happier place. Could you see yourself working for a great purpose in a dynamic, global culture? Then we might be a great match.
 



  





**Who are we looking for?** 




 Do you have a knack for delivering results through paid social media campaigns? Are you eager to support leading health and fitness coaches in scaling their businesses? We are looking for someone who wants to work closely with the most prominent health and fitness coaches, supporting them with creative concepts, planning, setting up, monitoring, and optimizing their paid social ads. You know how to build campaigns, write catchy ad texts, and crack performance data - but also how to communicate results and strategies to our coaches.In this role, and with our current and projected organizational growth, the opportunity to make an impact with Lenus is limitless!
 



  





  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Set up, monitor, and optimize paid social media campaigns to meet client goals across multiple platforms - mostly Meta.
* Analyze performance data daily, ensuring profitability while conducting weekly deep dives on results and trends.
* Stay current with social media and digital marketing best practices, leveraging insights to enhance campaigns.
* Communicate wins, challenges, and data insights effectively to clients and stakeholders, recommending future strategies.
* Collaborate with Key Account Managers and other teams to align on strategies, creative assets, and targets.
* Partner with coaches to support creative strategies and ensure that campaigns reflect their brand voice and goals.
* Manage budgets for your portfolio, driving growth and efficiency while hitting performance targets.



  





  





**Qualifications** 




 We value diverse experiences and backgrounds. While not all qualifications are mandatory, the ideal candidate should possess:
 


* 2+ years of hands-on experience with planning, buying, and executing paid social media campaigns.
* Fluent in Swedish and English.
* Experience developing creative concepts and executing engaging campaigns.
* Strong analytical skills with the ability to identify and optimize key performance metrics.
* Proficiency in Facebook Business Manager (extra points for experience with Email marketing, Google Ads, or additional digital funnels).
* A collaborative mindset, with excellent communication and teamwork skills.



  





 We encourage you to apply even if you don't tick all the boxes!
 **Please submit your application in english.** 




  





**The Team** 




 You’ll be part of our Nordic Marketing Team (Copenhagen, Sweden, Finland and Norway), a collaborative group of specialists dedicated to Paid Media, Branding, Websites, and Growth. We value teamwork, positivity, and building strong relationships. Our supportive culture ensures that everyone thrives and feels connected in their work.
 



  





 Locally, you will be part of the wider Stockholm team of Key Account Managers, Inbound Sales Representatives, and Brand/Performance Marketers, working closely together to deliver outstanding service and results. Based in the center of Stockholm, you will collaborate with colleagues across different functions, contributing to a dynamic and international work environment.
 



  





  





**Perks & Benefits** 




 At Lenus, we believe in rewarding our team members for their hard work and dedication. Here are some of the benefits you'll enjoy:
 


* Structured and prioritized development opportunities
* Development feedback cycle with quarterly check-ins
* Team events and monthly social events in all our offices
* Daily snacks and fuel. Our offices are set up with fruits, snack bars, and a selection of drinks
* A Macbook and other hardware to support you in your daily work



  





 We'll be going through applications frequently, so if this sounds like a great opportunity for you, hit apply and our Talent Acquisition Team will go through it and be in touch with you!
 **Please submit your application in english.** 




  





  





**About Lenus** 




 Lenus was founded in Denmark by Bertram Thorslund, in 2016. We are a health technology company that empowers health coaches to scale their businesses through the use of pioneering technology and our in-house business, health and innovation expertise.
 



 We’re inspiring a new generation of health coaches. People who have the power to move not only bodies, but minds too. We are at the forefront of an industry under rapid transformation and our strategic innovations pave the path for the change we want to see. Happier and healthier people.
 



 Today, Lenus is headquartered in Copenhagen, with offices in New York, London, Stockholm, Berlin, Hamburg, Madrid, Helsinki, Oslo, Toronto, Miami, Southern California & beyond.

title: Growth manager
description:
Nofence is a Norwegian scale up who has built the world’s first virtual fencing solution for livestock. As we aim to revolutionize livestock management we will need top-notch competence in our Growth Team, and we are now looking for a Growth Manager. Elevate your career with us and be at the forefront of innovation in agricultural technology!
   

  

**About The Role**
 Growth Marketing is a highly technical and strategic role responsible for the end-to-end digital customer journey. You will orchestrate the global "Growth Stack", combining AI-driven performance marketing, automated CRM lifecycles, champion growth products and advanced analytics.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
**Data driven experimentation**
 The core of the role is running a high-tempo "experimentation engine." You aren't just launching campaigns; you are testing hypotheses.
   

  

* A/B Testing: Testing landing pages, email copy, and pricing structures.
* Funnel Analysis: Identifying exactly where potential customers "drop off" (e.g., they sign up for a demo but never attend).


**Lead Generation & Acquisition**
* Manage and develop growth products: Be the champion of key growth products such as referral programs, lead generator programs etc.
* Performance Marketing: Managing paid spend on platforms like Meta and google Ads.
* Inbound Strategy: Collaborating with the creative studio to create "lead magnets". Analyze which visual hooks and narratives are converting and provide data-backed briefs for new asset iterations.
* Outbound Automation: Setting up automated sequences to warm up cold prospects before they hit a salesperson’s desk.
* AI Orchestration: Manage and steer automated bidding and targeting systems across Meta, Google, and emerging AI search engines (Perplexity/ChatGPT).
* Global Budget Stewardship: Dynamically allocate a multi million global budget across Norway, UK, US, and Spain based on real-time performance and regional priorities.


**Growth Automation**
* Nurture Architecture: Build and optimize automated email/SMS "nurture loops" in the CRM (e.g., HubSpot) to move leads from initial interest to collar installation.
* Lead Scoring & Routing: Develop models to identify high-intent prospects and trigger the correct "hand-off" to local sales or marketing teams.
* Customer Retention: Implement automated check-ins and referral programs to turn existing users into brand advocates.


**Analytics & Data Integrity**
* The "Source of Truth": Own the marketing attribution model. Implement server-side tracking and Conversion APIs to ensure data accuracy in a cookieless environment.
* Performance Reporting: Maintain automated dashboards for leadership, focusing on North Star metrics: CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost), LTV (Lifetime Value), and Payback Periods.


 Attribution: Determining which channels (LinkedIn, SEO, Webinars) actually drive high-quality leads, not just clicks.
   

  

**About You**
 The right candidate is preferred to have the following qualifications:
   

  

* Minimum 5 years in Growth, Performance Marketing, and/or Marketing Operations.
* Proven growth- or product management experience from a scale-up in international B2B, IoT, Ag-tech, or SaaS company.
* Budget Management: Experience managing multi-million NOK spends across diverse geographies and channels.
* Tech Stack Mastery: Expert-level knowledge of CRM systems (HubSpot/Salesforce) and Analytics tools (GA4, Looker, or Segment). Experience with developing attribution models.
* AI native: You are up to date on the latest tools and trends on AI within growth, marketing and sales, AEO and optimization experience is a plus.


**Core Competencies**
* Full-Stack Thinking: You see the "plumbing" (data/API), the "math" (ROI/CAC), and the "magic" (creative hooks) as one single system.
* Analytical Rigor: You are obsessed with clean data and finding the "why" behind the numbers.
* Technical Agility: You are comfortable working with developers on tracking implementations while also being able to write compelling technical briefs for creators.
* Autonomous & Proactive: You thrive in an environment of "local ownership" and high-pace execution.


**About Nofence**
 Managing livestock has always been a challenge. Humanity has tried everything from herding with dogs to walling the animals in with barbed wire, electric, and wooden fences, but these are costly and time-consuming solutions and often in conflict with the local wildlife. It has also left vast areas of potential pasture overgrown and underutilized.
   

  

 Until now.
   

  

 Enter virtual fences.
   

  

 What started as a small group of farmers and engineers with a big idea has become Nofence – the world’s first virtual fencing solution for livestock. Curious about how it works? Find out more here.
   

  

**About The Application**
